lede: A look at August 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Johann Wolfgang von Goethe and Taichang Emperor, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2022 Phoenix shooting
events.desc: Phoenix shooting: A man opens fire on pedestrians outside of a hotel in Phoenix, Arizona, resulting in the deaths of three people, including the perpetrator.

events.title: Pakistan
events.desc: Pakistan's National Assembly passes a constitutional amendment to make the "Qur'an and Sunnah" the "supreme law" but the bill is defeated in the Senate.

events.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina
events.desc: The autonomous Croatian Community of Herzeg-Bosnia in Bosnia and Herzegovina is transformed into the Croatian Republic of Herzeg-Bosnia.

events.title: Norrmalmstorg robbery
events.desc: Norrmalmstorg robbery: Stockholm police secure the surrenders of hostage-takers Jan-Erik Olsson and Clark Olofsson, defusing the Norrmalmstorg hostage crisis. The behaviours of the hostages later give rise to the term Stockholm syndrome.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Marseille and Toulon are liberated.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Royal Navy defeats the German fleet in the Battle of Heligoland Bight.

events.title: William Herschel
events.desc: William Herschel discovers a new moon of Saturn: Enceladus.

events.title: Bishops' Wars
events.desc: Second Bishop's War: King Charles I's English army loses to a Scottish Covenanter force at the Battle of Newburn.

events.title: Ferdinand II, Holy Roman Emperor
events.desc: Election of Ferdinand II, Holy Roman Emperor.

events.title: Ottoman–Portuguese conflicts (1538–1560)
events.desc: Turkish–Portuguese War: Battle of Wofla: The Portuguese are scattered, their leader Christovão da Gama is captured and later executed.

births.name: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
births.line: German novelist, poet, playwright, and diplomat

births.name: Ivo Josipović
births.line: Croatian lawyer, jurist, and politician, 3rd President of Croatia

deaths.text: Emmett Till, African-American kidnapping and lynching victim
Mu'in ad-Din Unur, Turkish ruler and regent
Orestes (father of Romulus Augustulus), Roman general and politician
Augustine of Hippo, Algerian bishop, theologian, and saint
